title: Almost Chess
special: White's Queen is replaced by a Chancellor (Rook + Knight compound).

title: Amazon Chess
special: Queens replaced by Amazons (Queen + Knight). The most powerful piece possible.

title: Antichess
win: Lose all pieces or get stalemated
special: Captures are mandatory. First to lose all pieces wins.

title: Atomic Chess
special: Captures cause explosions destroying all pieces on adjacent squares.

title: Breakthrough
special: Pawns only — first to reach the far side wins. No captures backward.

title: Capablanca Chess
special: Two extra pieces: Archbishop (B+N) and Chancellor (R+N). Wider board.

title: Chigorin
special: White's Bishops replaced by Knights. Four Knights vs standard army.

title: Codrus
special: No check exists. You win by getting your own King captured.

title: Courier Chess
special: Medieval German variant from the 1200s. Extra bishops and sage pieces.

title: Duck Chess
special: After each move, place the duck (blocker) on any empty square.

title: Endgame Chess
special: Only Kings and pawns. Pure endgame technique from move one.

title: Extinction Chess
win: Capture all of one piece type
special: Lose all your knights, bishops, rooks, or pawns — you lose the game.

title: Fischer Random (Chess960)
special: Back rank pieces randomised (960 positions). Castling rules adapted.

title: Five-Check
special: Extended Three-Check — five checks wins instead of three.

title: Fog of War Chess
special: You only see squares your pieces can move to. No check warnings.

title: Giveaway
special: Forced captures. King is not royal. Stalemate is a loss.

title: Grand Chess
special: Same new pieces as Capablanca, bigger board, pawns start on rank 3.

title: Horde Chess
win: White checkmates or Black destroys horde
special: White has a full army. Black has 36 pawns. Asymmetric survival.

title: King of the Hill
win: Checkmate or king reaches centre 4 squares
special: Moving your king to d4/d5/e4/e5 is an instant win.

title: Knightmate
special: Knight and King swap roles. The Knight is royal; Kings move as knights.

title: Los Alamos
special: First computer chess (1956). 6x6 board, no Bishops, no castling.

title: Maharaja & Sepoys
special: One player has a full army. The other has one piece that moves as queen + knight.

title: Makpong
special: King cannot move out of check. You must block or capture instead.

title: Marseillais Chess
special: Each player makes TWO moves per turn (except White's first turn).

title: Minichess
special: Gardner's 5x5 board. All piece types, fast tactical games.

title: Monster Chess
special: White moves twice per turn but starts with only King, Rooks, and pawns.

title: No Castling Chess
special: Standard chess with castling removed. Kings must develop naturally.

title: Pawns Only
special: Kings and pawns only. First to promote wins instantly.

title: Peasants' Revolt
special: White has King + 8 pawns vs Black's King + 2 Knights + 8 pawns.

title: Progressive
special: Moves per turn escalate: 1, 2, 3, 4... Check ends your turn early.

title: Racing Kings
special: No checks allowed. Both kings start on 1st rank. Race to the top.

title: Rifle Chess
special: Capturing pieces stay on their square — they 'shoot' the target.

title: Single-Check
special: One check wins. No checkmate needed — any single check ends the game.

title: Stalemate Wins
special: Standard chess but stalemate is a win, not a draw.

title: Standard Chess
special: Standard FIDE rules

title: Suicide Chess
special: Forced captures like Giveaway, but stalemate is a draw, not a loss.

title: Three-Check
special: First to check the opponent three times wins.

title: Torpedo Chess
special: Pawns can move two squares forward from any rank, not just the starting rank.

title: Upside-Down
special: Pieces start on the opponent's back rank. Instant tactical chaos.
